# Production

*Coming soon — lecture notes and materials for this chapter will be added as the course progresses.*

## Overview

Production theory analyzes how firms transform inputs (labor, capital, materials) into outputs. Just as consumer theory models rational consumers, production theory models profit-maximizing firms.

## Topics

### Production Functions

- The production function: $q = f(L, K)$
- Inputs and outputs
- Short run vs. long run
- Marginal product of labor ($MP_L$) and capital ($MP_K$)
- Average product of labor
- Law of diminishing marginal returns

### Isoquants

- Isoquants as the firm's equivalent of indifference curves
- Marginal rate of technical substitution (MRTS): $MRTS = MP_L / MP_K$
- Returns to scale: constant, increasing, and decreasing
  - CRS: $f(tL, tK) = t \cdot f(L, K)$
  - IRS: $f(tL, tK) > t \cdot f(L, K)$
  - DRS: $f(tL, tK) < t \cdot f(L, K)$

### Common Production Functions

- Cobb-Douglas: $f(L, K) = L^\alpha K^\beta$
- Perfect substitutes: $f(L, K) = aL + bK$
- Perfect complements (Leontief): $f(L, K) = \min\{aL, bK\}$

*Detailed notes will be posted here as the semester progresses.*
